references.title: Self-regulated learning strategies predict learner behavior and goal attainment in Massive Open Online Courses

extracted.Features: [Age, Gender: Female, Education: PhD, Education: Master’s, Education: Professional, Education: Bachelor’s, Occupation: Student, Occupation: Employed, Experience with topic, Online Courses Started, Online Courses Finished, Time Commitment, Complete all assesments, Complete all lectures, OLEI: Research Revelant, OLEI: Job Relevant, OLEI School Relevant, OLEI: Earn Certificate, OLEI: Prestigious uni./instru., OLEI: Meet new People, OLEI: Take with others, OLEI: Personal Growth, OLEI: Experience MOOC, OLEI:  Fun and Challenge, OLEI: Improve Spanish, OLEI: Career Change, OLEI: General Interest]
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Authors start by colleting data from surveys and then use correlation analysis to see which constructs are more conducive to different outcomes in the course. 

This analysis sub-sequently followed by a Regression analysis to see how each of the variables extracted actually contributes to each one of the different SRL constructs.
extracted.Cluster Description:
SRL constructs vary with motivation to enter the MOOC and with previous acaemic background. 

Certificate, Meet People, Prestige are all among the most relevant motivators for higher SRL skills.
extracted.Implications:
Goal Setting and Strategic Planning are especially relevant for success in MOOCs. 

LEarners with strong SRL skills tend to revisit course content to retest their learning. 

Help-seeking seems to have a negative relationship with success. These results are somewhat suprising… since prior literature usually points to success when there is motivation to work with learners - this may come from the fact that, while a study buddy may be an interesting approach, the fact is that online communitites usually don’t have a structured support system to foster that environment,
extracted.Comments:
Authors extract data from surveys over different MOOC students and see the effect of their answers on multiple SRL constructs.

They make multiple analysis over the validity of the answers… identify the most relevant predcitors of each SRL construct using a LASSO regression.
extracted.Limitations:
Lasso Regression only

Does not consider Behavioral Data

Potential problems with the validity of the constructs

MOOC-only context

annotations.text: Success in MOOCs requires good SRL skills. However, not all students have these skills developed. Thus it is important not only to be able to identify which SRL strategies should be promoted BUT to also identify which SRL are underdeveloped in the student population.

Authors of the paper use a sample of close to 5000 students and collect logs, survey answers and course achievement variables from them. The analysis of this data shows that Goal Setting and Strategic Planning are the most effective SRL skills to have to be successful while struggling students who seek help often underperform their peers.
More interestingly, the authors also found that multiple student characteristics such as motivation or demographics were predictive of SRL skills.
